name: Belvís de Monroy from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular via Bohonal de Ibor and the Embalse de Valdecañas
name_original: Belvís de Monroy desde Navalmoral de la Mata — circular por Bohonal de Ibor y el Embalse de Valdecañas
description: This moderate 32.4-mile road cycling route from Navalmoral de la Mata circles via Bohonal de Ibor, offering views of Embalse de Valdecañas a
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Navalmoral De La Mata traverse a landscape characterized by picturesque meadows, expansive plains, and serene pine forests. The region, part of the Campo Arañuelo, offers varied scenery for cyclists, including views of the Valdecañas Reservoir. Elevation changes are present, ranging from gentle gradients across plains to more significant climbs, providing diverse road cycling terrain. This area is well-suited for exploring the natural features of Extremadura on two wheels. Best road cycling routes around Navalmoral De La Mata The most popular road cycling route is Belvís de Monroy from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular via Bohonal de Ibor and the Embalse de Valdecañas, a 32.4 miles (52.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain and views of the Valdecañas Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pórtico de Curia de Talavera la Vieja desde Navalmoral De La Mata — circular por la Presa del Embalse de Valdecañas, a moderate 33.8 miles (54.4 km) path. This route offers scenic stretches along the Valdecañas Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Talayuela from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular by Tiétar, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with minimal elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Navalmoral De La Mata is defined by expansive plains, pine forests, and the presence of the Valdecañas Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate routes to more challenging distances. The routes in Navalmoral De La Mata are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Navalmoral De La Mata's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Navalmoral De La Mata?
A: Navalmoral De La Mata offers a substantial network of over 40 dedicated road cycling routes. These routes vary in length and elevation, catering to different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Navalmoral De La Mata?
A: The region features diverse terrain, from picturesque meadows and expansive plains to serene pine forests. You'll encounter routes with gentle gradients across the plains, as well as more significant elevation changes, particularly around areas like the Valdecañas Reservoir, providing varied challenges for road cyclists.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes near Navalmoral De La Mata?
A: Yes, some routes offer longer distances. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371355325/portico-de-curia-de-talavera-la-vieja-desde-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-por-almaraz-y-el-embalse-de-valdecanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pórtico de Curia de Talavera la Vieja desde Navalmoral de la Mata — circular por Almaraz y el Embalse de Valdecañas&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 120 km and includes over 1000 meters of elevation gain, making it suitable for those seeking a more challenging, longer ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy several cultural and natural attractions. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt;, offering commanding views. The remains of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga&lt;/a&gt; provide spectacular views of the Valdecañas reservoir. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granite Pillar of Almaraz&lt;/a&gt; is another point of interest.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Navalmoral De La Mata?
A: Many routes in the area are designed as circular loops, making them convenient for starting and finishing in Navalmoral De La Mata. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353259887/belvis-de-monroy-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-via-bohonal-de-ibor-and-the-embalse-de-valdecanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvís de Monroy from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular via Bohonal de Ibor and the Embalse de Valdecañas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353266333/portico-de-curia-de-talavera-la-vieja-desde-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-por-la-Presa del Embalse de Valdecañas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pórtico de Curia de Talavera la Vieja desde Navalmoral De La Mata — circular por la Presa del Embalse de Valdecañas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Navalmoral De La Mata?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn particularly pleasant for road cycling, with milder temperatures. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons are still viable. Winter offers cooler, often clear days, though some routes might be less crowded.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Navalmoral De La Mata?
A: The road cycling routes in Navalmoral De La Mata are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, scenic views of the Valdecañas Reservoir, and the opportunity to explore the natural beauty of Extremadura on two wheels.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: While many routes involve moderate distances and elevation, some sections or shorter routes can be suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353175443/talayuela-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-by-tietar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talayuela from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular by Tiétar&lt;/a&gt; route has minimal elevation gain, making it a gentler option for those looking for an easier ride.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes for a break?
A: Many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find local cafes and pubs to refuel and rest. The region is known for its hospitality, and you'll often find welcoming spots to take a break during your ride.

Q: Is there public transport or parking available for cyclists in Navalmoral De La Mata?
A: Navalmoral de la Mata is well-connected and serves as a strategic base. You can find parking options within the town. For public transport, the town has good communication links, including a train station, which could be a starting point for some routes or for reaching the area.

Q: What kind of gear is recommended for road biking in Navalmoral De La Mata?
A: Given the varied terrain, including both plains and areas with significant elevation, a road bike with a good range of gears is advisable. Standard road cycling attire, including a helmet, appropriate clothing for the season, and sufficient hydration, is recommended for comfortable and safe rides.
tips: Navalmoral de la Mata is a large rural farming town centrally located close to major highways and the Madrid-Lisbon railway line. The town was founded at the end of the 14th century as the Campana de la Mata farmhouse in the community of Villa y Tierra de Plasencia. Grain silos dominate the landscape and can be seen for miles.
Remains of a marble temple from an ancient Roman city called Augustobriga. Spectacular views of the Valdecañas reservoir and the bridge that crosses Guadalupe.
Bohonal de Ibor is a small town near the Valdecañas reservoir and the Tagus river. Stop at Plaza España to eat something and have a coffee and admire the Church of San Bartolomé Apóstol.

Belvís de Monroy is a small town in the Campo Arañuelo region, next to the Tagus River, near the entrance to the Monfragüe National Park. The medieval castle of Belvís el Monroy dominates the town dominating the surrounding landscape and can be seen from afar.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route is approximately 52.2 km (32.4 miles) long. Komoot estimates the average completion time to be around 2 hours and 44 minutes, but this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this route is best suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness. While it features mostly well-paved surfaces, there's an elevation gain of about 690 meters, indicating some climbs that might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Navalmoral de la Mata. You can typically find parking within the town center, such as near the Navalmoral de la Mata Town Center, which serves as a convenient starting point for the loop.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for race bikes. You'll cycle through the picturesque Extremadura region, which includes 'dehesas' (Mediterranean woodlands) with holm oaks and cork oaks, offering an easy-to-ride experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the unique 'Dehesa Extremeña' landscape, characterized by scattered holm and cork oaks. A significant feature is the Embalse de Valdecañas, offering scenic views of the reservoir. The route also passes through charming villages and historical areas.

faqs.question: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes by several points of interest. You can see the Castle of Belvís de Monroy, an imposing 13th-century fortress. Other notable sites include the Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga and the Church of San Bartolomé Apóstol in Bohonal de Ibor.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region can get very hot in summer. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the best times to cycle this route, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful landscapes as the flora changes.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Natural del Tajo, Camino de Monfragüe - Etapa 5, Camino de los Jerónimos - Etapa 3, and Camino de los Jerónimos - Etapa 4, among others.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in outdoor areas, this is a road cycling route primarily on paved surfaces and public roads. It's not typically designed with dog-walking in mind, and dogs would need to be able to keep pace with a bicycle or be carried safely. Always ensure your dog's safety and adhere to local leash laws.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths through the natural landscape of Extremadura.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Navalmoral de la Mata, Bohonal de Ibor, and Belvís de Monroy, you'll find opportunities for refreshments, cafes, and potentially accommodation. Navalmoral de la Mata, being the starting point, offers the most options.
